name: iam-principal-enumerator

metadata.name: iam-principal-enumerator
metadata.summary: CLI application that performs unauthenticated IAM principal enumeration against a target AWS account.

documentation:
# IAM Principal Enumerator

CLI application that performs unauthenticated IAM principal enumeration against a target AWS account.

The application accepts a custom wordlist containing principal names, and uses these to check for the existence of IAM principals in a target AWS account by attempting to update the trust policy of an attacker-controlled IAM role with the ARN of an IAM principal (user or role) in the target AWS account.

The error message received when updating the trust policy will determine if the IAM princiapl exists in the target account or not.

## Table of Contents

- [Installation](#installation)
- [Usage](#usage)
- [Arguments](#arguments)
- [Examples](#examples)
- [License](#license)

## Installation

1. Install the package using `pip`:

```sh
pip install iam-principal-enumerator
```

## Usage

To run the IAM Principal Enumerator, **ensure your terminal session is authenticated to your own attacker-controlled AWS account**, then use the following command:

```sh
iam-principal-enumerator <account_id> [options]
```

### Options

```plain
usage: iam-principal-enumerator [-h] [-r ENUM_ROLE_NAME] [-w WORDLIST] account_id

Enumerate valid IAM principals in an AWS account.

positional arguments:
  account_id            The target AWS account ID

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  -r ENUM_ROLE_NAME, --enum-role-name ENUM_ROLE_NAME
                        The name of the IAM role used for enumeration. The role name will
                        be suffixed with an 8-character random string.
  -w WORDLIST, --wordlist WORDLIST
                        Path to a wordlist to use when enumerating IAM principal names.
```

### Examples

Enumerate IAM principals using the default role name and wordlist:

```sh
iam-principal-enumerator 123456789012
```

Enumerate IAM principals using a custom role name and wordlist:

```sh
iam-principal-enumerator 123456789012 -r CustomRole -w /path/to/wordlist.txt
```

## License

This project is licensed under the MIT License. See the [LICENSE](./LICENSE) file for details.

modules.name: iam_principal_enumerator.main
documentation:
Main module for enumerating valid IAM principals in an AWS account.

This module provides the main functionality for enumerating valid IAM principals
in a specified AWS account. It includes functions for parsing command-line arguments,
validating inputs, creating and deleting IAM roles, and testing principal ARNs.

functions.name: iam_principal_enumerator.main.parse_args
documentation:
Parse command-line arguments.

```
return

Parsed arguments
```

functions.name: iam_principal_enumerator.main.main
documentation:
Main function to enumerate valid IAM principals in an AWS account.

- Parses arguments
- Validates inputs
- Creates an IAM role
- Tests principal ARNs
- Deletes the IAM role

modules.name: iam_principal_enumerator.aws.iam
documentation:
IAM utility functions.

This module provides utility functions for working with AWS IAM, including
validating ARNs, building ARNs, and creating IAM roles
and trust policies.

functions.name: iam_principal_enumerator.aws.iam.is_valid_arn
documentation:
Validate the AWS ARN.

```
param arn

AWS ARN to validate

return

True if valid, False otherwise
```

functions.name: iam_principal_enumerator.aws.iam.build_arn
documentation:
Build an AWS ARN.

```
param account_id

AWS account ID

param principal

Principal (e.g., root, user)

return

Constructed ARN string
```

functions.name: iam_principal_enumerator.aws.iam.create_role_trust_policy
documentation:
Create a trust policy for an IAM role.

```
param principal_arn

ARN of the principal

return

Trust policy as a JSON string
```

functions.name: iam_principal_enumerator.aws.iam.create_iam_role
documentation:
Create an IAM role with a trust policy for the current AWS account.

```
param role_name

Name of the IAM role to create

return

None
```

functions.name: iam_principal_enumerator.aws.iam.delete_iam_role
documentation:
Delete the specified IAM role.

```
param client

Boto3 IAM client

param role_name

Name of the IAM role to delete

return

None
```

functions.name: iam_principal_enumerator.aws.iam.is_valid_principal
documentation:
Test if the given ARN is a valid principal for assuming the role.

```
param client

Boto3 IAM client

param role_name

Name of the IAM role to test

param principal_arn

ARN of the principal to test

return

True if the principal can assume the role, False otherwise
```

modules.name: iam_principal_enumerator.aws.helpers
documentation:
Helper functions for generating ARNs and validating IAM principals.

This module provides functions to validate AWS account IDs, generate ARNs for IAM roles and users
based on principal names and to validate these ARNs using the AWS IAM client.

functions.name: iam_principal_enumerator.aws.helpers.generate_test_arns
documentation:
Generate ARNs for roles and users based on the principal names.

```
param account_id

AWS account ID

param principals

List of principal names

return

List of generated ARNs
```

functions.name: iam_principal_enumerator.aws.helpers.valid_principal
documentation:
Test a single ARN for validity.

```
param client

Boto3 IAM client

param role_name

Name of the IAM role to test

param arn

ARN to test

return

The ARN if valid, None otherwise
```

functions.name: iam_principal_enumerator.aws.helpers.is_valid_aws_account_id
documentation:
Validate that the AWS account ID is a 12-digit number.

```
param account_id

AWS account ID to validate

return

True if valid, False otherwise
```

modules.name: iam_principal_enumerator.aws.sts
documentation:
STS utility functions.

This module provides utility functions for working with AWS STS, including
retrieving the current AWS account ID.

functions.name: iam_principal_enumerator.aws.sts.get_current_account_id
documentation:
Get the current AWS account ID.

```
param client

Boto3 STS client

return

AWS account ID as a string
```

modules.name: iam_principal_enumerator.util
documentation:
Helper functions for various utility operations.

This module provides utility functions for generating random strings,
validating file existence, and reading lines from a file.

functions.name: iam_principal_enumerator.util.generate_random_string
documentation:
Generate a random string of the specified length.

```
param length

Length of the random string (default is 8)

return

A random alphanumeric string
```

functions.name: iam_principal_enumerator.util.is_valid_file
documentation:
Check if the given file exists.

```
param filename

Path to the file

return

True if file exists, False otherwise
```

functions.name: iam_principal_enumerator.util.read_lines_from_file
documentation:
Read lines from a file.

```
param filename

Path to the file

yield

Lines from the file, stripped of whitespace
```

functions.name: iam_principal_enumerator.util.print_results
documentation:
Print the results of valid principals.

```
param valid_principals

List of valid principal ARNs

return

None
```
